---
title: "Organizing Captured Documents"
---

---
title: "Organizing Captured Documents"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Capture/qdl1646248501126"
section: "Hyland Capture"
---

You can organize captured documents in the thumbnail viewer of the document review layout. Organizing may include classifying the documents, rearranging the pages within those documents, or deleting pages or documents.

To organize captured documents in a batch:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If there are no pending batches, capture documents to create a new batch (see Capturing Documents). The document review layout is displayed.
3.  In the thumbnail viewer to the right of the document viewer, set the focus to the page or document you want to organize by doing one of the following:
-
Click the page or document.

-
Use the arrow keys.

Both the focused page and the focused document are outlined in blue. In the following example, the focus is set to the first page of the first document:

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
Each document is displayed in the thumbnail viewer as a row, or document bar. The batch name is displayed in the upper-left corner of the viewer, and the numbers of documents and total pages in the batch are displayed in the upper-right corner of the viewer. The classification of each document is displayed in the upper-left corner of its document bar, and the number of pages in the document is displayed in the upper-right corner of its document bar.

Note: When pages in a document are still being processed, a progress spinner icon and text describing the action in progress (for example, Scanning Pages or Auto Classification) are displayed temporarily in the upper-left corner of the document bar. The actions are color coded: purple for document capture, orange for classification, and green for extraction. You cannot make changes to a document while it is undergoing bar code extraction (Auto Extraction).
When setting the focus, also note that unless you change the focus manually, it is set by default in the following ways:

-
If documents in the batch are still being captured, the focus is automatically set to the most recently captured page. If you changed the focus manually, you can reset the automatic focus back to the most recently captured page by clicking Return to Live in the upper-right corner of the document viewer.

-
Once a batch capture process has been completed or canceled, the focus is automatically set to the first page of the first document.

-
If you are scanning additional documents into the batch (see Scanning Additional Documents), the focus is automatically set back to the most recently captured page (without having to click Return to Live). Once the additional scanning is complete, the focus is set to the first page of the first additional scanned document.

-
If the Metadata Panel is displayed for indexing (see Indexing a Batch), the focus is set to the first blank, required metadata field on the focused document. If there are no blank, required metadata fields on the focused document, the focus is set to the first metadata field; if this first field already contains a value, the value is selected when the focus is set.

4.  To classify, reclassify, or unclassify the document, select a classification option from the classification drop-down list in the upper-left corner of the document bar. You can expand this drop-down list by clicking its arrow or pressing F7.
When reviewing document classification options, also note the following:

-
If the document class has been automatically assigned according to a bar code on the document, a bar code icon is displayed next to the classification drop-down list.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
If you make any changes to this document class assignment, the bar code icon is removed from the document bar.

-
If bar code extraction has been performed in conjunction with bar code classification, a red or green icon is displayed to the left of the classification drop-down list to indicate confidence levels in the assigned document class and metadata values (see Document Status Types for more information).

-
Shortcut keys are listed to the right of their assigned classifications in the classification drop-down list. Once you know the shortcut keys for the available classifications, you can use these shortcut keys to more quickly classify documents.

5.  Select the pages you want to organize by doing one of the following:
-
To select a specific page, set the focus to the page and then do one of the following:

-
Click the blue square in the upper-left corner of the page.

-
Press Enter.

-
To select multiple pages in the document, do one of the following:

-
For non-consecutive pages, hold Ctrl and click each page.

-
For consecutive pages, hold Shift and click the first and last pages in the series.

If the focus is already set to the first or last page you want to select, you can hold Shift and just click the page at the other end of the series.

-
To select all pages in the document simultaneously, do one of the following:

-
Select Select all pages from the separation options drop-down list. You can expand this drop-down list by clicking the vertical ellipsis icon in the upper-right corner of the document bar or pressing F9.

-
Press Alt + C.

A check mark is displayed in the upper-left corner of each selected page in the document, as shown in the following example.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
Note:
Pages can only be selected in the currently focused document. A selected page becomes deselected if you select the page again, select a separation option, or change the focus to a different document.

6.  To move the selected page or pages to a different position, drag the pages to the current position of another page in the batch. The dragged pages are moved to this position, and the other pages in the document are shifted left or right to make room for the dragged pages.
If you drag more than one page at a time, note the following:

-
The number of pages you are dragging is displayed over the thumbnail as you drag it.

-
The dragged pages retain their order when you drop them in the new position.

7.  To append the selected pages to the previous document in the batch, do one of the following:
-
To append a specific page or pages, select Append selected pages to above from the separation options drop-down list, or press Alt + P.

-
To append an entire document, select Append document to previous from the separation options drop-down list, or press Alt + A.

8.  To split pages from the current document into a new document, do one of the following:
-
To split all pages from the focused page to the end of the document, select Split from current page from the separation options drop-down list, or press Alt + S.

-
To split only the selected pages, select Split selected pages from the separation options drop-down list, or press Alt + L.

9.  To delete the selected pages from the batch, do one of the following:
-
To delete a specific page or pages, select Delete selected pages from the separation options drop-down list, or press Delete.

Note: If the document contains any bar code or blank pages that have been marked for deletion, each of these pages is outlined in red, and an indicator icon is displayed next to the document's total number of pages in the upper-right corner of the document bar. These marked pages are automatically deleted when you submit the batch.
-
To delete an entire document, select Delete document from the separation options drop-down list, or press Alt + Delete.

Note:
If you delete every document in the batch, the batch is also deleted.

For information on indexing the organized batch in the document review layout, see Indexing a Batch.

For information on submitting the organized batch for verification or export, see Submitting a Batch.
